articles.title: Karnataka HC Quashed Rule 86A Action as ITC Not Availed by Assessee but by Customer
articles.summary: The Karnataka High Court set aside a Rule 86A action taken against an assessee, ruling that the provision applies only when the assessee fraudulently avails Input Tax Credit (ITC). The court held that since the allegation pertained to the recipient's ITC and not the assessee's, the notice was without jurisdiction. The decision clarifies the scope of Rule 86A in GST compliance.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that Rule 86A applies only when the assessee fraudulently avails ITC. Since the allegation concerned the recipient’s ITC, the notice was without jurisdiction and was set aside. The post Karnataka HC Quashed Rule 86A Action as ITC Not Availed by Assessee but by Customer appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: No attachment of property as statutory tax charge was not a secured interest under IBC Amendment
articles.summary: The Tamil Nadu Value Added Tax (TNVAT) Department had attached a corporate debtor’s property under a statutory tax charge. However, the court ruled that such a tax charge does not qualify as a secured interest under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) Amendment. Consequently, the court directed the removal of the attachment. This ruling clarifies the legal treatment of tax claims in insolvency cases, affecting both tax authorities and corporate debtors.
articles.raw_summary: A statutory tax charge d noidt constitute a secured interest under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) therefore, the Court directed the removal of the TNVAT Department’s attachment over the corporate debtor’s property. The post No attachment of property as statutory tax charge was not a secured interest under IBC Amendment appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: NCLT Rejected CIRP Plea Due to Pre-Existing Dispute Over Delayed Charges Claim
articles.summary: The National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT) rejected a Corporate Insolvency Resolution Process (CIRP) plea because the claimant failed to provide sufficient records for delayed charges. The tribunal ruled that without invoices or ledger entries, the claim was disputed and unascertained, making it ineligible as operational debt under insolvency laws. The case highlights the importance of proper documentation in debt claims under India's insolvency framework.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether delayed charges without supporting records could qualify as operational debt. The Tribunal ruled that absence of invoices and ledger entries made the claim unascertained and disputed. The post NCLT Rejected CIRP Plea Due to Pre-Existing Dispute Over Delayed Charges Claim appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Supreme Court Earmarks SCBA Vice President Post For Women In 2026 Election
articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed that the Vice President post in the 2026 Supreme Court Bar Association (SCBA) elections be reserved for women advocates. A bench led by CJI Surya Kant and Justice KV Viswanathan passed this order to promote inclusivity. The SCBA Executive Committee has been asked to notify the Election Committee by May 29. This move aims to enhance women's representation in legal governance.
articles.raw_summary: In a progressive step towards inclusivity, the Supreme Court today earmarked the post of Vice President in the 2026 Supreme Court Bar Association's Executive Committee elections for women advocates. A bench of CJI Surya Kant and Justice KV Viswanathan passed the order, earmarking the post of VP, alongwith other EC posts, for women advocates in the impending 2026 elections of the SCBA. articles.title: Why quotas alone won’t increase women’s representation
articles.summary: The article examines why reservation (quotas) alone may not effectively increase women's representation in India, noting that existing social inequalities related to class and caste could limit its impact. It suggests that while quotas can help address underrepresentation, their effectiveness depends on broader structural factors. The analysis is framed within the context of India's policy landscape on gender representation.
articles.raw_summary: While reservation can address the issue of underrepresentation, its impact is likely to be shaped by existing inequalities related to class, caste etc

articles.title: Off-the-Shelf Software Not ‘Royalty’: SC Ruling Still Shapes Cross-Border Deals
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that payments for off-the-shelf software do not qualify as 'royalty,' removing Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) liability unless a permanent establishment exists. This decision impacts cross-border transactions involving software purchases. The ruling provides clarity on tax obligations for multinational companies operating in India. It eliminates ambiguity in tax treatment for such software payments.
articles.raw_summary: The ruling clarifies that payments for off-the-shelf software are not royalty. It eliminates TDS liability unless a permanent establishment exists. The post Off-the-Shelf Software Not ‘Royalty’: SC Ruling Still Shapes Cross-Border Deals appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: TDS Liability on LTC Cannot Arise When Deduction Barred by HC Interim Orders
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal ruled that no TDS liability arises under Section 201 when a taxpayer fails to deduct tax due to binding interim orders from a High Court. The decision clarifies that compliance with court directions prevents default liability. The ruling impacts tax compliance for entities subject to such judicial directives.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether failure to deduct TDS due to court directions attracts default. The Tribunal held that compliance with binding interim orders prevents liability under Section 201. The post TDS Liability on LTC Cannot Arise When Deduction Barred by HC Interim Orders appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: GST Assessment Set Aside as Order Passed Without Hearing: Madras HC Directs Fresh Order
articles.summary: The Madras High Court set aside a GST assessment order passed without giving the taxpayer an opportunity to be heard. The court directed tax authorities to issue a fresh order after considering the taxpayer's response and documents. This ruling underscores the importance of procedural fairness in tax assessments. The case involves a taxpayer challenging an assessment completed without their input.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the assessment confirmed without a reply warranted reconsideration. It remanded the case for fresh adjudication upon filing of response and documents. articles.title: Madras HC Quashes GST Assessment, Orders Fresh Adjudication on 50% Pre-Deposit
articles.summary: The Madras High Court set aside a GST assessment order issued without considering the taxpayer’s response to a show cause notice. The Court ordered a fresh adjudication but required the taxpayer to deposit 50% of the disputed amount upfront. This ruling emphasizes procedural fairness in tax assessments. The decision impacts how future GST assessments are conducted in India.

The Court quashed the order and allowed fresh adjudication subject to a 50% pre-deposit, emphasizing procedural fairness. The decision clarifies the jurisdiction of civil courts in such matters.

articles.title: BoJ policy decision Highlights: Bank of Japan keeps interest rate unchanged at 0.75%; 3 board members call for rate hike
articles.summary: The Bank of Japan (BoJ) kept its short-term interest rate target unchanged at 0.75%. However, three out of nine board members voted for a rate hike, citing concerns over inflationary pressures from the US-Iran conflict in the Middle East. This decision reflects growing worries about global economic stability and its potential spillover effects on India's trade and inflation dynamics.

articles.title: Reopening Invalid for Want of Proper Approval – ITAT Quashes U/s 148 Notice
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) invalidated a tax reassessment notice issued under Section 148 because the approval was obtained from the wrong authority. The tribunal ruled that compliance with Section 151 is a mandatory jurisdictional requirement, meaning improper sanction can nullify reassessment proceedings. This decision underscores the importance of procedural correctness in tax administration.

This decision impacts cooperative banks and taxpayers relying on this deduction.

articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

articles.title: On-Money Payment Addition Quashed as Excel Sheet & Third-Party Statements Found Unreliable
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal quashed an addition made to a taxpayer's income based solely on an Excel sheet and third-party statements, ruling that such evidence is unreliable without direct incriminating material. The tribunal emphasized that tax additions must be supported by concrete evidence connecting the assessee to alleged cash payments. This decision reinforces the requirement for robust proof in tax assessments and may impact future tax enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that additions cannot be sustained without incriminating material directly connecting the assessee to alleged cash payments. Reliance solely on third-party data was found insufficient. The ruling reinforces the need for concrete evidence in tax additions. The post On-Money Payment Addition Quashed as Excel Sheet   Third-Party Statements Found Unreliable appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Drawback Allowed as SEZ to DTA Supply Treated as Import Under Law: CBIC
articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) clarified that goods supplied from Special Economic Zones (SEZ) to Domestic Tariff Area (DTA) are treated as imports under the law. This means that re-exporting such goods qualifies for drawback benefits under Section 74 of the Customs Act. The decision ensures uniform treatment of SEZ clearances, impacting trade and tax policies for businesses operating in these zones.
articles.raw_summary: CBIC clarified that goods moving from SEZ to DTA are deemed imports. Hence, re-export of such goods qualifies for drawback under Section 74. The key takeaway is uniform treatment of SEZ clearances. The post Drawback Allowed as SEZ to DTA Supply Treated as Import Under Law: CBIC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: ‘No Addition, No Penalty’ – Section 271E Penalty Invalidated by ITAT Agra
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) in Agra ruled that a penalty under Section 271E of the Income Tax Act cannot stand if the underlying tax addition is deleted. The tribunal held that once the addition is removed, the penalty loses its legal foundation and must be cancelled. This decision impacts tax enforcement practices and clarifies the relationship between tax additions and penalties. The ruling was delivered in a case reviewed by ITAT Agra.
articles.raw_summary: he issue was whether penalty under Section 271E can stand after deletion of the underlying addition. The tribunal held that once the addition is deleted, the penalty loses its foundation and must be cancelled. articles.title: Security Deposits from Contractors Not Cash Credits: ITAT Upholds Deletion of ₹22.61 Cr Addition
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that security deposits from contractors should not be treated as unexplained cash credits under Section 68 of the Income Tax Act. The tribunal upheld the deletion of a ₹22.61 crore tax addition, stating these deposits were genuine trade liabilities. This decision clarifies tax treatment for contractor transactions and impacts how businesses report such deposits to tax authorities.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether contractor deposits could be treated as unexplained credits. The Tribunal held they were genuine trade liabilities, not taxable under Section 68. The ruling emphasizes the need for tangible new material to justify reopening closed assessments.

articles.title: Importance of 30th April for TDS on payment to Partners
articles.summary: Section 194T of India's Income Tax Act requires tax deducted at source (TDS) on payments made to partners, with a strict deadline of April 30 for deductions related to March transactions. Missing this deadline can result in interest charges and disallowance of the expense as a deduction. The rule applies to all partnerships and LLPs in India.

articles.title: IBC Proceedings Don’t Shield Directors from Cheque Bounce Prosecution: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that insolvency proceedings under the Insolvency and Bankruptcy Code (IBC) do not protect directors from prosecution for cheque bounce cases under Section 138 of the Negotiable Instruments (NI) Act. The court held that criminal liability for directors remains independent of insolvency proceedings, as both laws operate in separate legal spheres. This ruling clarifies the legal framework for directors' accountability in financial offenses.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court affirmed that insolvency proceedings under IBC do not bar prosecution under Section 138 of NI Act. It held that criminal liability of directors continues independently. Ruling clarifies that both laws operate in separate spheres. The post IBC Proceedings Don’t Shield Directors from Cheque Bounce Prosecution: SC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Striped hyena gets stronger global protection
articles.summary: The striped hyena has been granted stronger global protection under the Convention on the Conservation of Migratory Species of Wild Animals (CMS) treaty. This decision was made at the 15th Meeting of the Conference of the Parties (CMS CoP15). The species' conservation now requires greater cooperation among countries where it is found, including India. The treaty aims to protect migratory species threatened by habitat loss and human-wildlife conflict.

articles.title: MCA Expands Fast-Track Mergers, Easing Mid-Market Restructuring Beyond NCLT
articles.summary: The Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA) has introduced a new framework allowing more companies to qualify for fast-track mergers by replacing size-based criteria with debt thresholds. This reform aims to streamline approvals and reduce reliance on the National Company Law Tribunal (NCLT). The move is part of efforts to improve efficiency in mid-market corporate restructuring.

articles.title: S.27 Evidence Act | Joint Statement By Different Accused Admissible Only If They Lead To Distinct Discoveries: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that joint statements by different accused persons are admissible only if they lead to distinct discoveries. The Court observed that facts already discovered cannot be 'rediscovered' under Section 27 of the Indian Evidence Act. This ruling clarifies the evidentiary standards in criminal cases involving multiple accused individuals.
articles.raw_summary: There cannot be a "rediscovery" of a fact already discovered, the Court observed.

articles.summary: The Pune River Front Development Project is under investigation for allegedly violating environmental clearances by significantly narrowing the river's cross-section along its 44-km length, which could compromise its flood-handling capacity. The government is also prioritizing region-specific agricultural planning to address food security, higher farm incomes, better nutrition, and potential water shortages.

This ruling impacts how tax authorities and taxpayers handle provisional attachments in GST disputes.

articles.summary: The CBI is challenging a trial court order that discharged Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal, former deputy CM Manish Sisodia, and 21 others in the excise policy case. Kejriwal has refused to participate in the CBI's challenge before Justice Sharma, citing concerns over the lack of an impartial hearing. The case revolves around alleged irregularities in the Delhi government's excise policy. The outcome could impact legal precedents and governance norms in India.

articles.title: Expected credit loss rules: New provisioning framework to come into force in April 2027
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) will introduce new Expected Credit Loss (ECL) guidelines in April 2027, replacing the current incurred-loss-based provisioning framework with a staging framework for asset classification. The RBI will retain the existing 90-day overdue rule for classifying non-performing assets (NPAs). This change aims to improve risk assessment and provisioning practices in the banking sector.
articles.raw_summary: The new ECL guidelines would introduce a "staging framework" for asset classification under the ECL approach, replacing the existing incurred-loss-based provisioning framework. However, the central bank said in a note that it would retain the current 90-day overdue rule for classifying NPA.

articles.title: ITAT Upholds ₹5.97 Cr Addition Due to Non-Disclosure of TDS-Linked Contract Receipts
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) upheld a ₹5.97 crore tax addition against a taxpayer for not disclosing contract receipts linked to Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) in their income tax filings. The ruling clarifies that such receipts must be reported as income in the year they are received, even if not explicitly disclosed. The decision reinforces tax compliance requirements under Indian tax laws.
articles.raw_summary: The case examined whether contract receipts reflected in Form 26AS but not disclosed as income could be taxed. The Tribunal upheld the addition, ruling that failure to report such receipts in any year makes them taxable in the year of receipt. The post ITAT Upholds ₹5.97 Cr Addition Due to Non-Disclosure of TDS-Linked Contract Receipts appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Global Market: Emerging markets face mounting economic pressure amid Iran war disruptions
articles.summary: The ongoing war in Iran is causing economic disruptions globally, with emerging markets facing rising inflation, trade slowdowns, and higher borrowing costs. Commodity exporters may benefit, but many developing nations, including India, are experiencing currency depreciation and fiscal strain. The situation highlights vulnerabilities in global trade and energy supply chains, which could prompt policy responses from affected governments.
articles.raw_summary: The Iran war's economic ripple effect is hitting emerging markets hard, two months on. Rising inflation, trade disruptions, and fiscal strain are evident globally. While some commodity exporters see gains, many developing nations face currency depreciation and increased borrowing costs, demanding significant financial aid.

articles.title: Analysis-Iran war fertiliser squeeze could spell trouble for next year's grain harvests
articles.summary: Farmers worldwide face rising fertiliser prices due to the Iran war, risking next year's grain harvests. With grain prices low, many are rethinking planting plans, threatening global food production. The squeeze on fertiliser supplies, a key input for agriculture, could have far-reaching consequences for food security and trade policies.
articles.raw_summary: Farmers around the world are facing the second surge in fertiliser prices in four years due to the Iran war. But with grain prices too low to cushion the blow from the deeper supply crunch this time around, many are rethinking planting plans, putting global food production at risk.

articles.title: Jaggery-Water Curing Does Not Make Tobacco “Manufactured”: Madras HC
articles.summary: The Madras High Court ruled that jaggery-water curing does not transform tobacco into a 'manufactured' product for tax purposes. The decision ensures raw tobacco is taxed uniformly to avoid unequal treatment of identical goods. The court addressed whether processed tobacco should be taxed as a manufactured product or raw leaf, favoring the latter to prevent discrimination.
articles.raw_summary: The issue was whether processed tobacco should be taxed as raw leaf or manufactured product. The court held it as raw tobacco to avoid unequal tax treatment for identical goods. This 'China shock' raises concerns about unfair trade practices and their impact on global markets, including India's EV and automotive sectors.

New data showed China’s trade surplus – where its exports to the EU exceeded imports from the bloc – was $83bn (£61bn) in the first three months of 2026. Continue reading...

articles.title: Plastic litter and ghost nets threaten olive ridley hatchlings
articles.summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha is a critical nesting site for olive ridley sea turtles, with nearly 700,000 turtles arriving in February 2025 for mass nesting. Plastic waste and discarded fishing nets (ghost nets) pose severe threats to hatchlings, risking their survival. The article underscores the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect this endangered species and its habitat.
articles.raw_summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha’s Ganjam district is among the most important turtle rookeries in the world. Every year, hundreds of thousands of olive ridley sea turtles (Lepidochelys olivacea) converge here to lay eggs in a synchronous mass nesting event or arribada, with almost 700,000 olive ridleys coming ashore to lay eggs in February 2025 alone. articles.title: Digital lenders put small business on watch list

The warning highlights the growing risks posed by such technologies to India's digital infrastructure and national security.

The analysis highlights ongoing supply bottlenecks as a key factor influencing global oil prices.

The situation involves Iran, the US, and regional stakeholders, with potential global energy supply disruptions. India, a major oil importer, may face economic and policy implications due to these developments.

articles.title: Punjab activist climbs down after 18 months atop 400-ft BSNL tower as anti-sacrilege law notified

The law's notification followed prolonged public outrage over alleged desecration incidents. Khalsa's protest, which began in 2024, was a demand for justice in sacrilege cases. The new legislation marks a significant policy response to address religious sensitivities

articles.summary: Goldman Sachs has shared predictions about the future of the Strait of Hormuz, a key shipping route for global oil supplies. This could affect India's energy imports and economic stability, given its heavy reliance on oil from the region.
